text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. Readable code makes it easy for you and others to understand what the program does. One important practice is using clear naming conventions. Variables and functions should have names that describe their purpose, like `calculateTotal` instead of `ct`. Good code also breaks tasks into small, focused functions. Each function should do one thing well. This improves maintainability because you can fix or update a specific part without affecting the whole program. In short, clean code saves time, reduces errors, and makes collaboration much smoother.
